verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 is a canonical mitochondrial chaperonin residing in the mitochondrial matrix. However, it has well-documented cell-state-induced surface display in cancer cells and immune-activated contexts: tumor cells and stressed cells externalize HSPD1 to the outer leaflet as an immune-regulatory 'danger-associated molecular pattern' (DAMP) and target for NK / γδ T cell recognition. This ecto-HSPD1 pool is the basis for therapeutic interest (HSP60-targeted antibodies, CAR-T programs). The baseline compartment is mitochondrial-internal; the surface pool is stress / cancer-state-restricted.
